title: Psalms — Lesson 3: Sacred Properties and Ways of Recitation

sections.opening: Sit down, dear one, the candle is lit and we continue our journey into the world of Psalms. In the previous lesson we encountered the Kabbalistic structure of Psalms — 150 chapters corresponding to 150 sefirot. Today I will teach you something special: how to use the power of Psalms in a practical way. Reciting Psalms is not just prayer, it's a Kabbalistic action that creates real change in the spiritual worlds and in your physical life. There are special sacred properties here, Kabbalistic methods of reading that the Baal Shem Tov and the Chida transmitted to us — and each one creates an energetic channel for a different type of blessing. Come, let us learn not just how to read Psalms, but how to activate the power hidden within them in a precise and connected way.
sections.theoryA:
How does each person find their personal chapter in Psalms?

Each one of us has a special personal Psalm chapter — this is the tradition of the holy Baal Shem Tov. Every person recites the chapter corresponding to their age plus one. A 30-year-old will say chapter 31, a 25-year-old will say chapter 26. This is not just a beautiful custom — it's a deep Kabbalistic practice.

Your personal chapter creates a direct connection between you and the spiritual root of your soul in this year. Each year the soul ascends a level, and each Psalm chapter corresponds to a specific spiritual frequency. When you recite your chapter, you connect to the specific energy that your soul needs now for its correction and growth.

Many people ask me: "Maktuba, why specifically plus one?" The answer lies in the secret of pregnancy — we are not just our chronological age, but also the coming year that is already beginning to grow within us. The chapter of age plus one connects us to potential that has not yet manifested, to the power growing in the soul.

There is also a connection to practical Kabbalah here: every number is a sefirah, every sefirah is a channel of spiritual abundance. Your personal chapter is your direct channel for transmitting light from above to below. When you read it, you open a pipeline of blessing that is precisely directed to your soul at this point in time.

practice1.instruction: Calculate your personal chapter (age + 1) and read it now. Pay attention to which words or phrases connect with you more than others. Write down 2-3 sentences that catch your attention.

sections.theoryB:
How are Psalms connected to the months of the year and the zodiac signs?

Now I will teach you a beautiful connection that exists between Psalms and Kabbalistic astrology. Each Hebrew month has a special Psalm chapter that corresponds to the zodiac sign of the month and its spiritual energy. This is not just an external association — it's a precise map of cosmic energy flow throughout the year.

Nissan with the sign of Aries connects to chapter 107 — a chapter of redemption and hope, fitting for the month of freedom. Sivan with the sign of Gemini connects to chapter 119 — the longest chapter, fitting for the month of giving of the Torah when we received all knowledge. Tammuz with the sign of Cancer connects to chapter 137 — "By the rivers of Babylon", fitting for the month of exile and longing for home.

What's special about these connections is that they create a complete Kabbalistic calendar. When you read the chapter of the month, you connect to the cosmic stream flowing at this time. It's like tuning yourself to the right radio station — you receive the energy that the upper worlds are broadcasting in this period.

An additional connection: every zodiac sign is connected to a specific Kabbalistic sefirah, and every sefirah is connected to a Hebrew letter. When you read the chapter of the month, you are not just praying — you are performing a Kabbalistic unification of time, place and soul. You bring yourself into harmony with the cosmic rhythm.

practice2.instruction: Check which Hebrew month we are in now and find the corresponding Psalm chapter. Read it and notice how its energy feels different from your personal chapter. Pay attention to the connection between the zodiac sign and the content of the chapter.

sections.deepDive:
What's not written in the books: reading chapter 119 according to the letters of the Name

Here is a secret that the Chida wrote about as "a wonderful segulah beyond measure" — a special way to read chapter 119, the longest chapter in Psalms. This chapter is built from 22 sections, each section of 8 verses, and each section begins with a different letter from the Hebrew alphabet.

The secret is to read the verses that correspond to the letters of your name. If your name is "Yael", you would read the verses that begin with Yod (verses 73-80), with Ayin (verses 121-128), and with Lamed (verses 89-96). This creates a Kabbalistic name of your own within the holiest chapter in Psalms.

Why does this work? Because the letters of your name were not given to you by chance. Each letter carries a specific Kabbalistic power, and when you connect this power to chapter 119 — which is "Written Torah" in the Kabbalistic sense — you create a spiritual frequency unique to your soul.

I have seen women who did this exercise and suddenly everything changed for them — their spiritual state, inner clarity, even the synchronicities in life. This is not magic — it's spiritual technology. You tune into your soul's frequency through the holy names.

questions.question: Which Psalm chapter would a 45-year-old person read?
questions.explanation: According to the Baal Shem Tov tradition, every person reads the chapter corresponding to their age plus one. A 45-year-old would read chapter 46.

questions.question: Which Psalm chapter corresponds to the month of Sivan?
questions.explanation: The month of Sivan with the sign of Gemini connects to chapter 119 — the longest chapter, fitting for the month of giving of the Torah.

questions.question: How many verses are in each section of chapter 119?
questions.explanation: Chapter 119 is built from 22 sections, each section of 8 verses, each section beginning with a different letter of the alphabet.

questions.question: How do you read chapter 119 according to the letters of the name?
options: Read only the first verse of each letter
Read all 8 verses of each letter in the name
Read only the first letter of the name
Read the chapter in reverse order
questions.explanation: The Chida's segulah is to read all 8 verses that correspond to each letter in your name.

questions.question: What is the spiritual purpose of reading the personal chapter?
options: To connect to the energy the soul needs this year
questions.explanation: The personal chapter creates a connection to the specific energy that the soul needs now for its correction and growth in this year.

closing.summary: Today we learned about the practical power of Psalms — the personal chapter that connects each person to their soul energy, the monthly chapters that connect us to the cosmic stream, and the special segulah of chapter 119 that creates a personal Kabbalistic name. Psalms are not just beautiful words — they are a precise Kabbalistic tool for spiritual work.
closing.wisdom: "Your chapter is your key — use it every day."
closing.nextPreview: In the next lesson we will dive into the most powerful chapters — which chapters for danger, which for livelihood, and which for finding one's soulmate.

faq.question: What happens if I forget several times to read my personal chapter?
faq.answer: No problem! The spiritual connection is not damaged. Just start again when you remember. The soul understands the intention.

faq.question: Must I read the chapter in Hebrew or can I use a translation?
faq.answer: The Hebrew words carry the Kabbalistic power, but if you only understand translation — better with understanding than without. Intention and heart are the main thing.

faq.question: Can I read several monthly chapters together?
faq.answer: Yes! You can combine chapters — for example the chapter of the month with the personal chapter. This creates a special energetic combination.

faq.question: When during the year do the monthly chapters change?
faq.answer: On the new Hebrew month. Therefore it's important to know the Hebrew calendar and follow the transitions between months.

seo.title: Psalms: Sacred Properties and Ways of Recitation - The Practical Power of Psalms
seo.description: Learn about the personal chapter by age, monthly chapters by zodiac signs, and the segulah of chapter 119 by name letters. Tradition of the Baal Shem Tov and Chida for practical use.
